abstract: An identifier that specifies a complication if your app supports multiple complications per family.
overview:
## Discussion

In watchOS 7 and later, ClockKit represents a complication by its [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplication/family] and its [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplicationDescriptor]. Descriptors often represent categories of information that the complication can display. For example, a weather app may support `Condition`, `Temperature`, and `Precipitation` descriptors.

For apps created for watchOS 6 or earlier, the system automatically sets the configuration’s [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplication/identifier] property to [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKDefaultComplicationIdentifier] if you don’t implement your data source’s [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplicationDataSource/getComplicationDescriptors(handler:)] method. Similarly, the system sets the identifier to [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKDefaultComplicationIdentifier] if the complication came from a shared watch face, but the sender chose to exclude private information.

Because default identifiers can come from shared watch faces, your data source’s [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplicationDataSource/getCurrentTimelineEntry(for:withHandler:)] and [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplicationDataSource/getTimelineEntries(for:after:limit:withHandler:)] methods must check for the [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKDefaultComplicationIdentifier] value, and provide generic complication entries when it occurs.

# identifier

**Instance Property**

An identifier that specifies a complication if your app supports multiple complications per family.

## Declaration

```swift
var identifier: String { get }
```

## Discussion

In watchOS 7 and later, ClockKit represents a complication by its [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplication/family] and its [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplicationDescriptor]. Descriptors often represent categories of information that the complication can display. For example, a weather app may support `Condition`, `Temperature`, and `Precipitation` descriptors.

```swift
let ComplicationConditionIdentifier = "ComplicationTypeCondition"
let ComplicationTemperatureIdentifier = "ComplicationTypeTemperature"
let ComplicationPrecipitationPercentageIdentifier = "ComplicationTypePrecipitationPercentage"
```

For apps created for watchOS 6 or earlier, the system automatically sets the configuration’s [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplication/identifier] property to [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKDefaultComplicationIdentifier] if you don’t implement your data source’s [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplicationDataSource/getComplicationDescriptors(handler:)] method. Similarly, the system sets the identifier to [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKDefaultComplicationIdentifier] if the complication came from a shared watch face, but the sender chose to exclude private information.

Because default identifiers can come from shared watch faces, your data source’s [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplicationDataSource/getCurrentTimelineEntry(for:withHandler:)] and [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplicationDataSource/getTimelineEntries(for:after:limit:withHandler:)] methods must check for the [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKDefaultComplicationIdentifier] value, and provide generic complication entries when it occurs.

## Accessing Data About the Complication

- **family**: The family to which the complication belongs.
- **userActivity**: An object that represents the state of the app at a moment in time.
- **userInfo**: A dictionary of additional data associated with the complication.

items.description: The family to which the complication belongs.
